Is the user in the group that has M365 licenses assigned? Check Entra ID group membership.
User M365 licenses group లో member? Entra ID group membership check.
2
Check license seat availability
Seats available check
Does the organization have available license seats? All seats consumed = no auto-assignment.
Organization license seats available? All consumed = no auto-assign.
3
Check for license assignment errors
Assignment errors check
Entra ID > Licenses > All Products. Check for assignment errors. Common: service plan conflicts.
Entra ID > Licenses > All Products. Assignment errors. Service plan conflicts.
4
Manually assign or fix group
Manual assign or fix
If group-based is delayed: assign license directly. Or fix the licensing group and wait 10-15 min.
Group-based delayed: directly assign. Or fix group + wait.
5
Confirm access after propagation
Access confirm
Wait 5-10 min after assignment. Test Outlook, Teams, SharePoint.
5-10 min wait. Outlook, Teams, SharePoint test.
HR / Manager Answer
How to explain it
License issues block all Microsoft tools on Day 1. I check the licensing group, seat availability, and any assignment errors, then resolve so the new hire can work immediately.
License issues Day 1 all Microsoft tools block. Group, seats, errors check — resolve.
Real-World Organizational Example
🏢 30 Interns No Teams — License Plan Conflict After Org Switch
ConsultingGroup 30 interns Day 1 couldn't access Teams. All in 'Interns-M365' group but 'License Error: Service plan conflict'. Organization switched from M365 Business Basic to F3 but licensing group still had old plan. Fix: removed old plan, assigned F3 only. 20 minutes, all 30 working.
ConsultingGroup 30 interns Teams blocked. Old M365 Basic plan + new F3 conflict. Fix: old plan remove, F3 only. 20 min all working.
QAD4
An AD group sync to Entra ID is consistently missing some members. How do you diagnose and fix?
L3Entra ID ConnectOU Sync Scope
▼
TICKETINC-GRPSYNC-9201
PRIORITYP2
APPAD + Entra ID Connect
CLIENTIT Operations
Troubleshooting Steps
1
Check Entra ID Connect sync scope configuration
Entra ID Connect only syncs OUs and objects within its configured sync scope. If the missing group members are in an OU outside the sync scope, they will not appear.
2
Review group nesting depth
Entra ID Connect syncs group membership but has limits on nested group resolution depth. If members are in nested subgroups beyond the sync depth, they may be excluded.
3
Run a full sync and check for errors
In Entra ID Connect, run a Full Synchronization cycle: Start-ADSyncSyncCycle -PolicyType Initial. Check the sync log for any attribute-level errors for the missing members.
4
Check if missing members are filtered objects
Entra ID Connect can have object filtering rules. If a filter (e.g., 'only sync enabled accounts') excludes some group members, they will not sync.
5
Validate the Entra ID group membership after sync
After running a sync, verify the group membership directly in Entra ID admin. Compare against the AD group membership to confirm all expected members are now present.
HR / Manager Answer
How to explain it
Group sync gaps usually come from OU scope, object filtering, or nested group depth limits. I check the sync scope and filtering first before looking at individual user objects.
🏢 Real-World Example
🏢 Security Group Missing 40 Members — OU Not in Sync Scope
LegalFirm Entra ID security group for client system access was missing 40 members. Investigation: those 40 users were in the newly created 'Partners' OU which had not been added to the Entra ID Connect sync scope when the OU was created. Added the OU to sync scope, ran a full sync. All 40 members appeared in Entra ID within 10 minutes.
QAD5
On-premises AD accounts are getting locked out by a service or scheduled task after a password change. How do you find the offending source?
L3Account LockoutService Account
▼
TICKETINC-SVC-LOCK-9202
PRIORITYP2
APPActive Directory
CLIENTIT Security
Troubleshooting Steps
1
Enable AD audit logging for account lockout events
In AD, enable Account Lockout auditing in the Default Domain Controller Policy. This logs the source IP and process name that caused the lockout.
2
Check the PDC Emulator for lockout source
Account lockout events are recorded on the PDC Emulator domain controller. Run: Get-ADUser username -Properties LockedOut, LastBadPasswordAttempt to find when and where lockouts occur.
3
Use Microsoft Account Lockout Tools
Download the Microsoft Account Lockout and Management Tools. The LockoutStatus.exe tool shows which DC recorded the bad password attempts and from which source.
4
Search for the old password in service accounts and scheduled tasks
After a user changes their password, search for services running under their account: Task Scheduler, Windows Services, IIS app pools, mapped drives. Any using the old password will lock the account.
5
Update or migrate the service to use a managed service account
Once the offending service is found, update its credentials. Long-term: migrate services to Group Managed Service Accounts (gMSA) which auto-rotate passwords and never lock out.
HR / Manager Answer
How to explain it
Service account lockouts after password changes are one of the most common AD support issues. I trace the lockout source via audit logs and PDC Emulator, find the service with the old password, and migrate it to gMSA.
🏢 Real-World Example
🏢 Finance User Locked Out Every Monday — SQL Server Job Had Old Password
ManufacturingCo Finance user locked out every Monday at 7 AM. Checked PDC Emulator lockout logs: source was the internal SQL Server. A weekly SQL Agent job was running under the Finance user's credentials. After the user changed their password, the SQL job kept retrying with the old password every minute. Credentials updated in SQL Agent. Migrated the job to use a gMSA. Zero lockouts since.
QAD6
How do you clean up thousands of stale Active Directory accounts that are no longer in Workday?
L3 SeniorAD CleanupStale Accounts
▼
TICKETPROJ-CLEANUP-9203
PRIORITYL3
APPAD + Workday
CLIENTIAM Engineering
Troubleshooting Steps
1
Build a reconciliation report: Workday active workers vs AD accounts
Export active workers from Workday. Export all enabled AD accounts. Compare the two lists. Any AD account not in the Workday active list is a candidate for cleanup.
2
Exclude service accounts, shared accounts, and admin accounts from the cleanup
Not every AD account without a matching Workday record is stale. Service accounts, shared mailboxes, and admin accounts do not have Workday records. Categorize carefully before disabling anything.
3
Move candidates to a 'Stale-Review' OU and disable — do not delete
Never delete accounts immediately. Move to a designated OU, disable, and leave for 30 days. If no complaints arise, the account was truly unused. Delete after 30 days.
4
Send a report to each department manager for validation
Before disabling any account, send the list to the relevant manager for sign-off. This catches accounts that exist for legitimate reasons not reflected in Workday.
5
Automate ongoing reconciliation to prevent future accumulation
Build a monthly automated reconciliation that identifies any AD account not in Workday that has been inactive for 90+ days and routes it to the cleanup queue.
HR / Manager Answer
How to explain it
Stale accounts are a security risk and compliance finding. I do a phased cleanup — disable first, validate for 30 days, delete after — and then automate ongoing reconciliation to prevent the problem from recurring.
🏢 Real-World Example
🏢 4,200 Stale AD Accounts Found and Cleaned Up — Including 3 With Active Logins
HealthSystem IAM audit found 4,200 AD accounts with no matching Workday record. After categorizing: 3,800 confirmed stale, 400 were service/shared accounts. The 3,800 were disabled. 30 days later: 12 complained — those 12 reactivated (legitimate cases not in Workday). 3,788 deleted. Shockingly: 3 of the stale accounts had active logins within the past month — investigated as potential unauthorized access. Monthly reconciliation now automated.
QAD7
Entra ID Connect is running but no changes are syncing from AD to Entra ID. How do you diagnose?
L3Entra ID ConnectSync Troubleshooting
▼
TICKETINC-NOSYNC-9204
PRIORITYP2
APPEntra ID Connect
CLIENTInfrastructure
Troubleshooting Steps
1
Check the Entra ID Connect sync service status
On the Connect server: open Services.msc and confirm 'Microsoft Azure AD Sync' is running. If stopped: start it and watch for errors.
2
Check the sync scheduler status in PowerShell
Run: Get-ADSyncScheduler. Check that SyncCycleEnabled is True and NextSyncCycleStartTimeInUTC shows a recent/upcoming time.
3
Look for errors in Synchronization Service Manager
Open the Synchronization Service Manager on the Connect server. Check the Operations tab for any recent import or export errors with error codes.
4
Check the Entra ID Connect server's outbound connectivity
The Connect server must reach Entra ID's endpoints. Run a connectivity test: Test-AzureADConnectivityTest or manually test https://login.microsoftonline.com from the server.
5
Review disk space and memory on the Connect server
Sync failures can be caused by resource exhaustion. Check disk space (sync logs can fill the drive) and memory usage on the Connect server.
HR / Manager Answer
How to explain it
Connect sync stops usually come from the sync service being stopped, the scheduler being disabled, a connectivity issue, or resource exhaustion on the server. I check all four in sequence.
🏢 Real-World Example
🏢 Friday Night Sync Stopped — Connect Server Ran Out of Disk Space
RetailNetwork Monday morning: no user changes had synced since Friday. Entra ID Connect sync log files had accumulated 60 GB over 3 months and filled the OS drive. Service stopped itself. Fix: cleared old logs, restarted service, ran delta sync. Added log rotation policy: purge logs older than 30 days. Disk monitoring alert added at 80% capacity.
QAD8
How do you implement tiered admin accounts (Tier 0/1/2) in Active Directory to reduce privilege escalation risk?
L3 SeniorTiered AdminAD Security
▼
TICKETDESIGN-TIER-9205
PRIORITYL3 Senior
APPActive Directory
CLIENTSecurity Architecture
Troubleshooting Steps
1
Understand the three tiers of privilege
Tier 0: Domain Controllers and identity infrastructure. Tier 1: Servers and applications. Tier 2: Workstations and end-user devices. Tier 0 admins should ONLY log into Tier 0 systems.
2
Create separate admin accounts per tier
Each privileged user gets three accounts: a daily-use standard account, a Tier 2 admin account for workstation management, and (if needed) a Tier 1 or Tier 0 account for server/DC admin.
3
Enforce the tier boundaries with Group Policy and Authentication Policies
Use AD Authentication Policies and Silos to prevent Tier 0 accounts from logging into Tier 1 or Tier 2 systems and vice versa. This stops lateral movement from compromised lower-tier machines.
4
Protect Tier 0 accounts with PAWs (Privileged Access Workstations)
Tier 0 admin tasks should only be performed from dedicated hardened PAW devices — no browsing, no email, minimal software. PAWs reduce the attack surface for credential theft.
5
Audit and alert on any Tier boundary violations
Configure Windows Event Log alerts: any Tier 0 account used to log into a non-Tier 0 system should generate an immediate security alert.
HR / Manager Answer
How to explain it
Tiered administration reduces the blast radius of compromised credentials. Even if a Tier 2 workstation admin account is stolen, the attacker cannot reach domain controllers because Tier 2 accounts are blocked from Tier 0 systems.
🏢 Real-World Example
🏢 Tier Model Implementation Stopped Simulated Ransomware from Reaching Domain Controllers
FinanceCorp ran a red team exercise. Without Tier model: attacker compromised a helpdesk workstation, harvested admin credentials, reached a domain controller in 4 hours. After implementing the Tier model with authentication policies: same attack stopped at Tier 2 boundary. Helpdesk admin credentials were blocked by policy from accessing Tier 1 servers. Attacker was contained to a single workstation. Tier model implemented across all 15 sites.
QAD9
Entra ID PIM is not sending MFA challenges when activating privileged roles. How do you fix?
L3Entra ID PIMSecurity Gap
▼
TICKETINC-PIM-9206
PRIORITYP2
APPEntra ID PIM
CLIENTSecurity Ops
Troubleshooting Steps
1
Check the PIM role activation settings
In Entra ID > PIM > Azure AD Roles > [Role] > Settings. Confirm that 'Require Azure MFA on activation' is enabled. If unchecked, PIM will not prompt for MFA.
2
Verify the user has an MFA method registered
PIM MFA challenge uses the user's registered Entra ID MFA methods. If the user has no method registered, the MFA challenge cannot be issued.
3
Check if the user's Conditional Access policy is bypassing MFA
If a CA policy is set to skip MFA for this user (e.g., they are in a trusted location exclusion), PIM's MFA requirement may be bypassed.
4
Enable Entra ID Authentication Strength for PIM
Use the newer Authentication Strength feature in PIM: set a minimum authentication strength (e.g., phishing-resistant MFA) rather than just requiring 'any MFA'.
5
Test the full PIM activation flow after changes
Have a test admin activate their PIM role and confirm the MFA challenge appears. Verify the challenge uses the correct factor (push, FIDO2, etc.).
HR / Manager Answer
How to explain it
PIM without MFA challenge is a critical security gap — privileged roles should always require fresh MFA at activation. I check the role setting, user registration, and CA policy interactions to ensure MFA is genuinely enforced.
🏢 Real-World Example
🏢 Global Admin PIM Activation With No MFA — CA Policy Exclusion Was the Cause
CloudOrg security review found Global Admin PIM role was activating without MFA prompt. Investigation: the 3 Global Admins were in a 'Senior-Leaders-CA-Exclusion' group that bypassed all CA MFA requirements — including PIM challenges. Group removed. PIM 'Require MFA' setting confirmed enabled. All subsequent Global Admin activations now challenge MFA. Alert added: any PIM activation without MFA event = P1 security alert.
QAD10
How do you design a Group Policy structure that is maintainable as the organization grows?
L3 SeniorGroup PolicyAD Architecture
▼
TICKETDESIGN-GPO-9207
PRIORITYL3
APPActive Directory GPO
CLIENTInfrastructure Architecture
Troubleshooting Steps
1
Follow the LSDOU application order deliberately
GPO applies in order: Local, Site, Domain, OU. Design your OU structure so security policies at the Domain level cover all users, and exceptions are handled at OU level.
2
Use purpose-named GPOs, not catch-all GPOs
One GPO should do one thing: 'Win10-Password-Policy', 'Win10-Firewall-Config', 'Restricted-USB-Policy'. Avoid giant GPOs that do 50 things — impossible to troubleshoot or audit.
3
Use Security Filtering to target GPOs precisely
Apply GPOs to the right objects using Security Filtering (by AD group) rather than linking the same GPO to multiple OUs. This makes it easier to add/remove scope without editing the OU structure.
4
Test all GPO changes in a staging OU first
Before applying any GPO change to production, test it in a dedicated 'GPO-Test' OU with a small set of test machines. Catch errors before they affect 5,000 workstations.
5
Document every GPO with a description and owner
Use the GPO's 'Description' field. Note what it does, when it was created, who owns it, and when it was last reviewed. Undocumented GPOs are the most dangerous legacy configurations.
HR / Manager Answer
How to explain it
Good GPO design is like good code — modular, named clearly, tested before deployment, and documented. Badly structured GPOs become unmaintainable and are a major source of security drift.
🏢 Real-World Example
🏢 Legacy GPO Audit Found 40 Conflicting Policies — 12 Years of Undocumented Changes
UniversityIT AD environment had 180 GPOs after 12 years of growth. Many were undocumented, many conflicted. An IAM/infrastructure review took 3 months: documented all 180, consolidated 95 redundant ones, deleted 22 unused ones. Remaining 63 GPOs given a clear name, description, owner, and review schedule. GPO change tickets now mandatory — no undocumented GPO changes allowed.
QAD11
Users report that their AD-synced email address in Entra ID is wrong. How do you investigate and correct it?
L2Attribute SyncEntra ID Connect
▼
TICKETINC-EMAIL-9208
PRIORITYP2
APPAD + Entra ID Connect
CLIENTIT / Email
Troubleshooting Steps
1
Identify the source of the email attribute in AD
In AD, the primary email address can come from the mail attribute or the proxyAddresses attribute. Check which one Entra ID Connect is configured to sync.
2
Compare the AD attribute value to the Entra ID value
Check the AD user's mail and proxyAddresses attributes. Compare them to the userPrincipalName and mail attribute in Entra ID. Identify exactly which attribute is wrong.
3
Update the email attribute in AD — Entra ID is not the authoritative source
For synced users, changes must be made in AD first. Entra ID will overwrite any manual changes on the next sync. Fix the source, not the destination.
4
Check for attribute precedence conflicts if multiple sync rules exist
If Entra ID Connect has multiple sync rules, there may be an attribute precedence conflict. Check the Synchronization Rules Editor for the mail attribute precedence order.
5
Trigger a delta sync and verify the change propagated
After fixing the AD attribute, run a delta sync. Verify the email address in Entra ID now shows the correct value.
HR / Manager Answer
How to explain it
For synced users, the email address must always be fixed in Active Directory. Changing it directly in Entra ID will be overwritten at the next sync. I fix the source attribute in AD and verify the sync propagates the correct value.
🏢 Real-World Example
🏢 Email Address Wrong in Entra ID — Fixed in Entra ID But Kept Getting Overwritten
GlobalInsurer user's email in Entra ID kept reverting to an old maiden name after updates. IT kept fixing it in Entra ID admin, but it reverted every night. Root cause: AD still had the old email in the mail attribute. Entra ID Connect was overwriting the manual fix every sync. Fixed the mail attribute in AD. Sync propagated correctly. One-time fix — never reverted again.
QAD12
How do you handle a situation where two different AD forests need to be joined and identity conflicts must be resolved?
L3 SeniorForest MergerMulti-Forest
▼
TICKETDESIGN-FOREST-9209
PRIORITYL3 Senior
APPAD Multi-Forest + Entra ID
CLIENTInfrastructure Architecture
Troubleshooting Steps
1
Audit both forests: users, groups, and overlapping UPNs
Before any merger, inventory both forests. Look for: duplicate UPNs, duplicate email addresses, overlapping group names, conflicting password policies, and differing OU structures.
2
Design the target identity model: which forest is authoritative?
Decide: will one forest be dominant or will both be merged into a new forest? The authoritative forest's identity data takes precedence in conflict resolution.
3
Resolve UPN and email conflicts before the merger
Users with the same UPN or email in both forests will cause sync failures. Resolve by appending a domain-specific suffix (user@companyA.com vs user@companyB.com) before merge.
4
Use Entra ID's MetaVerse or a dedicated IGA tool to map identities across forests
For cloud identity: Entra ID Connect supports multi-forest sync with source anchor matching. Configure the sourceAnchor attribute (typically objectGUID or employeeID) to match accounts across forests.
5
Run the merger in phases with parallel access during transition
Phase 1: sync both forests to Entra ID in read-only mode. Phase 2: identify and resolve all conflicts. Phase 3: cut over authentication to the primary forest. Phase 4: decommission the secondary.
HR / Manager Answer
How to explain it
Forest mergers are some of the most complex IAM projects. I start with a thorough inventory of both forests, resolve conflicts systematically, and run the merger in phases to minimize risk.
🏢 Real-World Example
🏢 Post-M&A Forest Merger: 800 Identity Conflicts Resolved Over 90 Days
AcquiringBank acquired a smaller bank with its own AD forest. 800 UPN conflicts found between the two forests. Strategy: acquired bank users moved to a new UPN suffix (user@acquiredbank.com). Both forests synced to Entra ID Connect. SSO enabled for all apps from day 30. Secondary forest maintained for 90 days then decommissioned. Zero user disruption during the entire merger.
QAD13
How do you identify and remediate accounts with excessive Active Directory permissions discovered in a security audit?
L3Privilege CleanupSecurity Audit
▼
TICKETSEC-AUDIT-9210
PRIORITYP2
APPActive Directory
CLIENTSecurity Audit
Troubleshooting Steps
1
Export all privileged group memberships for audit
Pull membership of all high-privilege groups: Domain Admins, Enterprise Admins, Schema Admins, Account Operators, Backup Operators. Any account in these groups requires justification.
2
Apply the principle of least privilege to each finding
For each over-privileged account: what does this user actually need to do? Do they need Domain Admin or just local admin on specific servers? Reduce to the minimum required.
3
Remove unnecessary high-privilege group memberships
Remove accounts from groups they no longer need. For operational admin tasks, implement Entra ID PIM or AD delegation — temporary access instead of permanent membership.
4
Implement Just-In-Time admin access via PIM or PAM solution
Use Entra ID PIM for cloud admin roles and a PAM solution (CyberArk, BeyondTrust) for on-premises AD admin access. Permanent admin group memberships are replaced with time-limited activations.
5
Schedule quarterly privilege access reviews
Privileged access reviews must happen quarterly. Any account with Domain Admin rights must be re-certified by the CISO every quarter.
HR / Manager Answer
How to explain it
Excessive AD permissions are the number one path attackers use for lateral movement and privilege escalation. I identify over-privileged accounts, reduce them to least-privilege, and implement JIT access to eliminate standing privilege.
🏢 Real-World Example
🏢 Security Audit Finds 45 Users in Domain Admins — Should Be 3
RetailGroup security audit: 45 users in Domain Admins. Should be 3 (IAM team leads only). Investigation: 42 had been added for temporary projects over the years and never removed. All 42 removed immediately. 3 legitimate Domain Admin accounts moved to Entra ID PIM with 4-hour activation window. Quarterly Domain Admin membership review scheduled with CISO sign-off requirement.
← Back to Okta Guide
🚨
Interview Prep › P1 Security Incidents
P1 Security Incidents
P1 భద్రతా సంఘటనలు
Priority 1 Response
QP1A
Executives are locked out of Microsoft 365 during business travel due to Conditional Access. What do you do?
Business travel లో Executives Conditional Access వల్ల Microsoft 365 లో locked out అయ్యారు. మీరు ఏమి చేస్తారు?
P1L3Conditional AccessExecutive
▼
TICKETINC-P1-AAD-55801
PRIORITYP1
APPMicrosoft 365
CLIENTLeadership Team
Troubleshooting Steps
ట్రబుల్‌షూటింగ్ దశలు
1
Identify blocked sign-ins
Blocked sign-ins identify చేయండి
Pull Entra ID sign-in logs, filter by the executive's UPN and look for CA policy name blocking access. Identify the specific condition failing.
Entra ID sign-in logs pull చేసి executive's UPN ద్వారా filter చేసి CA policy name చూడండి. Specific condition failure identify చేయండి.
2
Temporary exclusion — scoped and time-bound
Temporary exclusion — scoped మరియు time-bound
Add the executive to a temporary exclusion group for the blocking CA policy. Set a calendar reminder to remove exclusion in 24 hours.
Blocking CA policy కోసం executive ని temporary exclusion group లో add చేయండి. 24 గంటల్లో exclusion remove చేసే calendar reminder set చేయండి.
3
Validate legitimacy
Legitimacy validate చేయండి
Confirm with the executive or their EA that the travel is legitimate before applying any exclusion.
Exclusion apply చేయడానికి ముందు executive లేదా వారి EA తో travel legitimate అయిందా confirm చేయండి.
4
Confirm MFA enforcement intact
MFA enforcement intact అయిందా confirm చేయండి
Ensure the exclusion only removes the location/device block, not MFA. MFA must always remain enforced.
Exclusion location/device block మాత్రమే remove చేస్తుందా, MFA కాదా ensure చేయండి. MFA ఎప్పుడూ enforced గా ఉండాలి.
5
Long-term fix: risk-based access
Long-term fix: risk-based access
Replace geo-blocking with risk-based Conditional Access. Use Entra ID Identity Protection risk signals instead of country blocks.
Geo-blocking ని risk-based Conditional Access తో replace చేయండి. Country blocks కాకుండా Entra ID Identity Protection risk signals వాడండి.
HR / Manager Answer
HR / Manager సమాధానం
How to explain it
ఎలా వివరించాలి
Executives being locked out is a business-critical P1. My immediate response is to restore access quickly with a scoped, time-bound exclusion — never removing MFA. Long-term, I replace blunt geo-blocking with intelligent risk-based access so travel no longer causes outages.
Executives locked out business-critical P1. Immediate response: scoped, time-bound exclusion తో quickly access restore చేయడం — MFA ఎప్పుడూ remove చేయకూడదు. Long-term, blunt geo-blocking ని intelligent risk-based access తో replace చేస్తాను.
Real-World Organizational Example
నిజ జీవిత సంస్థ ఉదాహరణ
🏢 Org Example 🏢 సంస్థ ఉదాహరణ  Board Members Locked Out During Annual Investor Meeting  Annual Investor Meeting లో Board Members Lock Out
PublicCo CFO, CEO, and two board members were presenting via Teams from Singapore when they were all suddenly locked out — a new CA policy blocking 'high-risk locations' had gone live that morning. IAM on-call was paged at 9 AM EST (10 PM Singapore). Resolution in 7 minutes: Created emergency exclusion group 'CA-Travel-Exec-Exclusion'. Added all 4 users. Set removal reminder for 48 hours. Verified MFA still required. Board meeting continued. Post-incident: The geo-block policy was replaced with Entra ID Identity Protection risk-based CA — no more country blocks. New policy: if risk is Low + MFA completed = allow, regardless of country. Zero exec travel incidents in 18 months since.
PublicCo CFO, CEO, మరియు రెండు board members Singapore నుండి Teams ద్వారా present చేస్తున్నారు, అందరూ suddenly locked out అయ్యారు — ఆ morning 'high-risk locations' block చేసే new CA policy live అయింది. IAM on-call 9 AM EST (Singapore 10 PM) కు page. 7 నిమిషాల్లో resolution: Emergency exclusion group 'CA-Travel-Exec-Exclusion' create. 4 users add. 48 గంటల removal reminder. MFA still required verify. Board meeting continued. Post-incident: Geo-block policy ని Entra ID Identity Protection risk-based CA తో replace. 18 నెలల్లో zero exec travel incidents.
QP1B
An audit discovers a user had Global Admin access for 3 days without approval. How do you handle this?
Audit లో user 3 రోజులు approval లేకుండా Global Admin access కలిగి ఉన్నారని కనుగొనబడింది. ఎలా handle చేస్తారు?
P1 AuditL3PIMLeast Privilege
▼
TICKETSEC-P1-77340
PRIORITYP1
APPMicrosoft Entra ID
CLIENTAudit & Security
Troubleshooting Steps
ట్రబుల్‌షూటింగ్ దశలు
1
Remove privileged role immediately
Privileged role వెంటనే remove చేయండి
Remove the Global Admin role without delay. Don't wait for the audit meeting — every minute of exposure is a risk.
Global Admin role delay లేకుండా remove చేయండి. Audit meeting కోసం wait చేయకండి — exposure ప్రతి నిమిషం risk.
2
Rotate credentials if needed
Credentials rotate చేయండి if needed
If the user performed any admin actions, rotate service account passwords and API keys they may have accessed.
User admin actions చేసి ఉంటే, వారు access చేసిన service account passwords మరియు API keys rotate చేయండి.
3
Review audit logs for actions taken
Actions taken కోసం audit logs review చేయండి
Check all admin activity during the 3-day window: any users created/deleted, policy changes, app registrations, or role assignments.
3-day window లో admin activity అన్నీ check చేయండి: users created/deleted, policy changes, app registrations, role assignments.
4
Identify assignment source
Assignment source identify చేయండి
Was it a PIM failure, manual error, or automation bug? Identify the exact path by which Global Admin was assigned.
PIM failure, manual error, లేదా automation bug? Global Admin assign అయిన exact path identify చేయండి.
5
Implement Privileged Identity Management
Privileged Identity Management implement చేయండి
Ensure all admin roles use PIM with time-bound activation, MFA requirement, and dual approval. No standing admin access.
అన్ని admin roles time-bound activation, MFA requirement, మరియు dual approval తో PIM వాడాలి. Standing admin access లేదు.
6
Report and document for audit
Audit కోసం report మరియు document చేయండి
Write an incident report: what happened, when detected, what actions were taken, what was the impact, what preventive controls are now in place.
Incident report రాయండి: ఏమి జరిగింది, ఎప్పుడు detected, ఏ actions తీసుకున్నారు, impact ఏమిటి, ఇప్పుడు ఏ preventive controls ఉన్నాయి.
HR / Manager Answer
HR / Manager సమాధానం
How to explain it
ఎలా వివరించాలి
Unauthorized admin access is a security incident with potential regulatory implications. I remove the role immediately, review every action taken during the exposure window, identify the control gap, and implement PIM to enforce just-in-time access going forward. The incident is fully documented for the audit.
Unauthorized admin access regulatory implications తో security incident. Role వెంటనే remove చేసి, exposure window లో తీసుకున్న ప్రతి action review చేసి, control gap identify చేసి, forward లో just-in-time access enforce చేయడానికి PIM implement చేస్తాను. Incident audit కోసం fully documented.
Real-World Organizational Example
నిజ జీవిత సంస్థ ఉదాహరణ
🏢 Org Example 🏢 సంస్థ ఉదాహరణ  Accidental Global Admin During Help Desk Ticket  Help Desk Ticket లో Accidental Global Admin
InsuranceFirm security team ran a weekly privileged access review and found a Tier-1 help desk analyst had Global Admin for 72 hours. The analyst had been trying to reset a user's password. They should have used the Help Desk Administrator role, but a copy-paste error in a Terraform script assigned Global Admin instead. During the 72 hours, the analyst had unknowingly viewed the tenant's conditional access policies and app registrations (no changes). IAM response: (1) Role removed in 2 minutes. (2) Audit log reviewed — no destructive actions found. (3) PIM enforced for all admin roles requiring 2-person approval. (4) Terraform scripts for role assignments now require peer code review before deployment. Documented as SOX control finding — remediated within 5 business days.
InsuranceFirm security team weekly privileged access review లో కనుగొంది — Tier-1 help desk analyst కు 72 గంటలు Global Admin. Analyst user's password reset చేయడానికి try చేసారు. Help Desk Administrator role use చేయాలి, కానీ Terraform script లో copy-paste error Global Admin assign చేసింది. 72 గంటల్లో analyst అడియగా CA policies మరియు app registrations చూసారు (changes లేవు). IAM response: (1) 2 నిమిషాల్లో role remove. (2) Audit log — destructive actions లేవు. (3) అన్ని admin roles కు 2-person approval తో PIM. (4) Role assignment Terraform scripts కు deployment ముందు peer code review. SOX control finding గా documented — 5 business days లో remediate.
QP1C
Global MFA failure across Okta — all users can't authenticate. What is your response?
Okta global MFA failure — all users authenticate చేయలేకపోతున్నారు. Response?
P1L3MFA Outage
▼
TICKETINC-P1-OKTA-88921
PRIORITYP1
APPOkta + All SaaS
CLIENTEnterprise IT
Troubleshooting Steps
1
Triage: one factor or all MFA?
Triage: scope determine
Is it all MFA factors or one (e.g., Okta Verify push only)? All users or a specific group? Scope determines urgency.
All MFA factors లేదా one factor? All users లేదా specific group? Scope = urgency.
2
Check Okta service status
Service status check
Check trust.okta.com and internal monitoring for service degradation or proxy issues.
trust.okta.com + internal monitoring. Degradation check.
3
Freeze recent IAM changes
Recent changes freeze
Pause any in-flight IAM changes. A policy or network zone change may have caused this.
In-flight IAM changes pause. Policy/zone change cause?
4
Enable backup factors for affected users
Backup factors enable
If Okta Verify push down, direct users to TOTP. If all push down, enable SMS/email OTP for trusted networks only — temporarily.
Push down → TOTP. All push down → SMS/email OTP trusted networks temporarily.
5
Identify and fix root cause
Root cause fix
Common: expired certificate, rate limiting, network zone blocking push servers, recent CA change. Fix specific cause.
Expired certificate, rate limiting, network zone, CA change. Specific fix.
6
Restore and do post-incident review
Restore + post-incident
Re-enable policies. Confirm auth across all user groups. Write post-incident report in 24 hours.
Policies re-enable. Auth confirm. 24h post-incident report.
HR / Manager Answer
How to explain it
A company-wide MFA outage is P1 — every employee is impacted. I restore authentication safely, keep leadership informed, and document root cause afterward.
Company-wide MFA outage P1 — every employee. Safely restore, leadership inform, root cause document.
Real-World Organizational Example
🏢 3000 Users: Push Cert Expired Over Weekend
FinancialServices Corp 3,000 employees Monday morning — Okta Verify push not working. Push notification signing certificate expired over weekend. 8:05 AM: expired cert identified. 8:12 AM: all users directed to TOTP. 8:15 AM: cert renewed. 8:30 AM: push restored. Total outage: 30 minutes. Added: 30/60/90-day cert expiry alerts.
FinancialServices 3,000 push blocked. Cert expired. TOTP interim. Cert renewed. 30 min outage. 30/60/90-day cert alerts added.
Q16
CA policy change blocks legacy auth — on-premises SMTP app stops working. How do you handle?
CA policy change legacy auth block — on-prem SMTP app stop. ఎలా handle?
P1Legacy Auth
▼
TICKETINC-AAD-25991
PRIORITYP1
APPLegacy SMTP
CLIENTInfrastructure
Troubleshooting Steps
1
Confirm CA is the cause
CA cause confirm
Check Entra ID sign-in logs. Confirm failure reason: 'Legacy authentication blocked'.
Sign-in logs. 'Legacy authentication blocked' confirm.
2
Identify all legacy auth users
All legacy auth identify
Use Entra ID workbook 'Sign-ins using legacy authentication' to find all affected users and apps.
'Sign-ins using legacy authentication' workbook. All affected find.
3
Create time-limited exception
Time-limited exception
Add affected service account to CA exclusion group. Set end date — not permanent.
Affected service account CA exclusion. End date — not permanent.
4
Plan app modernization
App modernization plan
Work with app owner to upgrade to Modern Auth/OAuth. SMTP Basic Auth is a security risk.
App owner: Modern Auth/OAuth upgrade. SMTP Basic = risk.
5
Set deprecation timeline
Deprecation timeline
30/60/90 day timeline for modernization. Exception removed after deadline regardless.
30/60/90 day modernization. Exception removed at deadline.
HR / Manager Answer
How to explain it
Legacy auth bypasses MFA — it's a known attack vector. I create a time-limited exception and work with the app team to modernize.
Legacy auth MFA bypass. Time-limited exception + app modernization.
Real-World Organizational Example
🏢 Finance SMTP Relay Breaks — Legacy Auth Block
AuditFirm blocked legacy auth company-wide. Finance SMTP relay (Basic Auth) stopped — 300 invoices queued. 48-hour exception for SMTP service account while upgrading to Microsoft Graph API OAuth 2.0. Exception removed after 2 days. No legacy auth exceptions >72 hours since.
AuditFirm legacy auth block. Finance SMTP Basic Auth stop. 300 invoices queued. 48h exception. Graph API OAuth upgrade. Exception removed.
QP1D
Entra ID Connect sync failure prevents password sync for hundreds of users. What is your approach?
Entra ID Connect sync failure hundreds of users password sync block. ఏమిటి approach?
P1Entra ID Connect
▼
TICKETINC-P1-SYNC-44019
PRIORITYP1
APPAD + Entra ID
CLIENTInfrastructure
Troubleshooting Steps
1
Check sync service on Connect server
Sync service check
Windows Services on Connect server: is 'Microsoft Azure AD Sync' running? Check event log for errors.
Connect server Windows Services: 'Microsoft Azure AD Sync' running? Event log errors.
2
Restart if safe — don't force if errors
Restart if safe
Restart if stopped without errors. If restart fails, investigate failure reason first before forcing.
Stopped without errors → restart. Restart fails → investigate first.
3
Communicate workaround to Service Desk
Workaround communicate
Tell Service Desk: users can try SSPR for cloud password, or wait for sync restoration.
Service Desk: SSPR cloud password, or wait.
4
Diagnose root cause
Root cause diagnose
Common: service account password expired, disk space full, Windows update conflict, certificate expiry.
Service account password expired, disk full, Windows update conflict, certificate.
5
Run delta sync after fix
Delta sync after fix
Start-ADSyncSyncCycle -PolicyType Delta. Verify users can authenticate after sync.
Start-ADSyncSyncCycle Delta. Auth verify.
6
Add monitoring + redundancy
Monitoring + redundancy
Entra ID Connect Health alerts if sync older than 3 hours. Consider staging sync server.
Connect Health: sync >3 hours alert. Staging server consider.
HR / Manager Answer
How to explain it
Sync failure directly blocks users. I restore the service quickly, communicate a workaround, fix root cause, and add monitoring.
Sync failure users block. Quick restore, workaround, root cause, monitoring.
Real-World Organizational Example
🏢 1800 Users Monday Lock-Out: Disk Full Killed Friday Night Sync
AutomationCorp sync server ran out of disk Friday night (growing sync logs). Monday morning: 1,800 users couldn't log into cloud apps. 63-hour sync gap. Fixed: cleared 40 GB old logs, restarted service, ran delta sync. All 1,800 working by 8:25 AM. Added: disk alert at 80%, auto-purge logs after 30 days, staging sync server.
AutomationCorp Friday night disk full. Monday 1,800 users blocked. 63h gap. Cleared logs, restart, delta sync. 8:25 AM all working. Disk 80% alert + staging server.
QP1F
A phishing attack has captured credentials for 20 users — all have logged in from a suspicious IP. What is your immediate response?
P1 SecurityL3Phishing Response
▼
TICKETSEC-PHISH-9301
PRIORITYP1
APPOkta + All Apps
CLIENTSOC / Identity
Troubleshooting Steps
1
Force sign-out and revoke all sessions for all 20 users immediately
In Okta admin, revoke all active sessions for every impacted account. This terminates all currently open application sessions across every connected app.
2
Force password reset for all 20 accounts
Require an immediate password reset for all 20 users. Use the admin-initiated reset so users are forced to change on next login.
3
Temporarily block the suspicious IP in Okta network zones
Add the suspicious IP to an Okta deny list or blacklist network zone. This blocks any further authentication attempts from that IP.
4
Review the Okta System Log for every action those 20 accounts took
Pull a full audit log for each of the 20 accounts since their suspicious login. What apps did they access? Did they export data? Did they change any settings?
5
Notify affected users and provide re-enrollment guidance
Contact each user directly (phone, not email — email may be compromised). Confirm it is them. Guide them through password reset and MFA re-enrollment.
6
Escalate any data access findings to the CISO and legal team
If any sensitive data was accessed during the window, this becomes a potential data breach — notify the CISO and legal team immediately for incident response.
HR / Manager Answer
How to explain it
A credential phishing incident requires immediate containment: revoke sessions, force password resets, and audit every action taken by the impacted accounts. Speed matters — every minute of active session is potential damage.
🏢 Real-World Example
🏢 20 Users Phished — Sessions Revoked, Data Access Audited, Zero Breach
FinTech SOC detected 20 users logging in from the same IP in Nigeria within 10 minutes — all had clicked a phishing link. IAM revoked all 20 sessions within 4 minutes of alert. Password resets forced. IP blocked. Audit log review: 18 of 20 had no sensitive data access. 2 had opened but not downloaded finance reports. CISO notified. No data breach declared. Post-incident: phishing simulation training for all 20 users, MFA enforced to FIDO2 for all.
QP1G
A Conditional Access policy was accidentally applied to all users including the Global Admin account — now no one can log in. How do you recover?
P1 LockoutL3 SeniorCA PolicyBreak-Glass
▼
TICKETINC-CA-LOCKOUT-9302
PRIORITYP1
APPEntra ID + M365
CLIENTSecurity Ops
Troubleshooting Steps
1
Use the break-glass account to regain admin access
Break-glass accounts must be excluded from ALL CA policies by design. Log in with the break-glass account using its hardware key. This is exactly what they exist for.
2
If break-glass doesn't work: use the Azure Portal via a direct tenant URL
Try accessing https://portal.azure.com/?Microsoft_AAD_IAM_enableGroupAndUser=true as a last resort. Some portal access may not be subject to CA policies in certain emergency paths.
3
Once in: immediately modify or delete the problematic CA policy
Find the policy that caused the lockout. Either disable it, modify the scope to exclude admin accounts, or delete it entirely. Confirm the change saves successfully.
4
Test admin login with a non-break-glass admin before declaring resolved
Have a regular admin (non-break-glass) attempt to log in. Confirm they can now authenticate successfully before closing the incident.
5
Implement safeguards: CA policy What-If test before every change
Going forward: ALL CA policy changes must be tested using the CA What-If tool before being enabled. Test the change against both admin accounts and standard users.
HR / Manager Answer
How to explain it
Locking out all admin accounts via a CA policy is one of the worst IAM incidents — you lose control of the entire tenant. Break-glass accounts exist for exactly this moment. After recovery, CA policy change process must include mandatory What-If testing.
🏢 Real-World Example
🏢 All Global Admins Locked Out — Break-Glass Account Restores Access in 8 Minutes
CloudCo junior IAM engineer deployed a CA policy scoped to 'All Users' without excluding admin accounts. All 4 Global Admins immediately locked out. Break-glass account used — regained access in 8 minutes. Faulty policy deleted. New policy created correctly with admin exclusion. Policy: CA changes now require peer review + What-If tool test before activation. Junior engineer given additional training on CA policy scope.
QP1H
An Okta administrator account shows login activity from two countries simultaneously — possible account takeover. How do you respond?
P1 ATOL3 SeniorAdmin Account Takeover
▼
TICKETSEC-ADMIN-ATO-9303
PRIORITYP1
APPOkta
CLIENTSOC / CISO
Troubleshooting Steps
1
Immediately suspend the admin account
Do not investigate first — suspend immediately. An admin account under active attack has the keys to your entire identity infrastructure.
2
Revoke all sessions and API tokens for that admin
In Okta, revoke the admin's active sessions AND review and revoke any API tokens that admin created. Attackers create API tokens early to maintain access even after password change.
3
Review all changes made by that admin in the last 24-48 hours
Pull the Okta System Log filtered by that admin's user ID. Look for: new admin accounts created, policy changes, new API tokens created, new trusted IPs added.
4
Roll back any unauthorized changes found
For each unauthorized change found: remove unauthorized admin accounts, revert policy changes, remove rogue trusted IPs. Document each rollback with a timestamp.
5
Verify the admin's identity via out-of-band channel before reactivating
Call the real admin on their personal mobile number. Verify it was them (it was not). Only reactivate after full identity verification and device security review.
HR / Manager Answer
How to explain it
An admin account compromise is the most severe IAM incident. I suspend first without hesitation, revoke all access artifacts, audit every change made by the account, and roll back anything unauthorized.
🏢 Real-World Example
🏢 Okta Admin Compromise: 3 Rogue API Tokens Created Before Detection
InsuranceTech SOC detected impossible travel: Okta admin authenticated from London and Singapore within 2 hours. Admin was confirmed in London only. Suspended account. Audit: attacker had created 3 API tokens. All 3 revoked. No policy changes found — attacker had just gained a foothold. All admin accounts moved to FIDO2 hardware keys. SOC alert threshold for admin impossible travel reduced from 4 hours to 30 minutes.
QP1I
During a merger, you need to give 300 acquired company employees access to your systems within 48 hours. How do you approach this safely?
P1 PriorityL3 SeniorM&A Onboarding
▼
TICKETPROJ-MERGER-9304
PRIORITYP1 Priority
APPOkta + All Apps
CLIENTM&A Integration
Troubleshooting Steps
1
Create a separate acquisition onboarding group — do not mix with employee population yet
Create a dedicated Okta group for acquired users. This allows controlled access assignment, easy audit, and clean rollback if needed.
2
Provision minimum required access only — least privilege from day one
Give acquired users only what they genuinely need on day one: email, communication tools, and their specific role-required applications. Do not grant broad access.
3
Enforce MFA enrollment before any access is granted
All 300 acquired users must complete MFA enrollment before their first login to your systems. No exceptions — even for executives.
4
Conduct a mini identity security review before provisioning
Verify with the acquired company's IT: are there any active security incidents, compromised accounts, or known malware on their devices? Do not onboard potentially compromised identities.
5
Assign a provisioning owner from both companies to coordinate
One person from your IAM team and one from the acquired company's IT must coordinate the onboarding. Clear responsibility prevents gaps.
6
Plan for a full role-based access review within 30 days
Day-one access is provisional. Within 30 days, managers must certify that each acquired employee has the correct access for their new role.
HR / Manager Answer
How to explain it
M&A identity onboarding must be fast but secure. I provision least-privilege access, enforce MFA from the first login, and treat day-one access as provisional — requiring a full review within 30 days.
🏢 Real-World Example
🏢 300 Acquired Employees Onboarded in 36 Hours — All MFA-Enrolled, Zero Security Incidents
AcquiringBank needed to onboard 300 acquired employees in 48 hours after close. IAM created a dedicated onboarding group, provisioned email + Zoom + role apps only. MFA enrollment required before first login. Security review of acquired company IT confirmed no active incidents. All 300 onboarded in 36 hours. 30-day access review completed: 45 access adjustments made. Zero security incidents during onboarding.
QP1J
A privileged service account password expired in production, causing an automated process to fail and impacting 500 users. How do you respond and prevent recurrence?
P1L3Service AccountgMSA
▼
TICKETINC-SVC-EXP-9305
PRIORITYP1
APPService Account + Okta
CLIENTIT Operations
Troubleshooting Steps
1
Identify the exact service account and reset its password immediately
Check Windows Event Log or application logs for the authentication failure. Identify the service account, reset the password immediately in AD.
2
Update the password in all locations that use it
The new password must be updated everywhere the account is used: Windows Services, Task Scheduler, IIS app pools, scripts, CI/CD pipelines. Missing one location re-triggers the failure.
3
Test the affected automated process after update
Restart the service or run the automated job manually. Confirm it succeeds end to end before declaring resolved.
4
Audit all other service accounts for upcoming expiry
Run a PowerShell audit: Get-ADUser -Filter {ServicePrincipalNames -like '*'} | Select Name, PasswordLastSet, PasswordNeverExpires. Find every account with an expiring password.
5
Migrate service accounts to Group Managed Service Accounts (gMSA)
gMSAs auto-rotate their passwords every 30 days. No human manages the password, no human forgets to rotate it, no automation breaks on expiry. This is the permanent fix.
HR / Manager Answer
How to explain it
Service account password expiry causing production outages is entirely preventable. The immediate fix is a password reset and update everywhere. The permanent fix is migration to gMSA which eliminates the problem entirely.
🏢 Real-World Example
🏢 ETL Pipeline Down for 3 Hours — Service Account Password Expired at Midnight
DataWarehouse nightly ETL pipeline failed at midnight. 500 analysts arrived to missing data reports. Investigation: the service account password had expired at 12:01 AM. IT reset password, updated in all 6 locations the account was used. ETL re-run. Data available by 5 AM. Audit: found 12 other service accounts with expiring passwords in the next 30 days. All 12 migrated to gMSA within 2 weeks. Zero service account expiry incidents since.
QP1K
A Conditional Access policy report-only mode shows thousands of users would be blocked if the policy were enabled. How do you safely enable it?
L3CA Policy RolloutChange Management
▼
TICKETPROJ-CA-ENABLE-9306
PRIORITYP2
APPEntra ID CA
CLIENTSecurity Project
Troubleshooting Steps
1
Analyze the report-only mode results by category
In Entra ID CA Insights, categorize the would-be blocked users: how many are blocked due to MFA not enrolled, device compliance, location, or risk? Each category needs a different pre-fix action.
2
Fix blockers before enabling — don't enable a policy that blocks legitimate users
For users who would be blocked due to missing MFA: run an MFA enrollment campaign first. For device compliance issues: extend Intune enrollment. Fix each category before enabling.
3
Enable in segments — start with a pilot group
Enable the policy first for a low-risk segment: IT staff or a small department. Monitor for 2 weeks. Expand to the next segment. Never enable for all users simultaneously.
4
Maintain an exclusion group for genuine exceptions
Create an emergency exclusion group. Any user who has a legitimate business reason to be excluded during the rollout can be added with a documented justification and time limit.
5
Set a full enablement target date and communicate it
Tell the business: this policy will apply to all users by [date]. Give 30 days of warning. Clear deadlines drive compliance.
6
Monitor the first 48 hours of each segment enablement closely
After each enablement segment, monitor CA sign-in logs intensively for 48 hours. Have IAM on-call to handle any blocked-user calls quickly.
HR / Manager Answer
How to explain it
Enabling a CA policy that would block thousands of users is a planning and communication project, not just a button click. I fix the root causes of the blocks first, then roll out in segments with monitoring at every step.
🏢 Real-World Example
🏢 CA Policy Rollout: From 3,200 Would-Be-Blocked to Zero in 6-Week Campaign
HealthOrg planned to enable a 'Require MFA for All Cloud Apps' policy. Report-only mode showed 3,200 of 5,000 users would have been blocked — mostly due to missing MFA enrollment. 6-week enrollment campaign: IT conducted department-by-department MFA enrollment drives. Policy enabled in 4 segments over 6 weeks. By enablement date: only 23 users remained without MFA — all were on authorized leave. Zero unplanned blocks on go-live day.
QP1L
Splunk receives no identity events from Okta for 6+ hours — the SOC is blind. What is your response?
P1L3SIEM BlindspotSplunk
▼
TICKETINC-SIEM-9307
PRIORITYP1
APPOkta + Splunk
CLIENTSOC / IAM
Troubleshooting Steps
1
Confirm Okta itself is generating events
Log into Okta admin and check System Log directly. If Okta logs show events but Splunk shows nothing, the pipeline is broken — not Okta.
2
Check the Splunk Add-on for Okta for authentication errors
In Splunk, check the Okta Add-on's data input status. Look for authentication errors or API failures. An expired API token or rotated token is the most common cause.
3
Verify the Okta API token used by Splunk is still valid
Check the Okta API token configuration in the Splunk Add-on settings. If the token was rotated or expired, generate a new token and update Splunk.
4
Check network connectivity between Splunk and Okta API
Confirm the Splunk server can reach the Okta API endpoint (https://[tenant].okta.com/api/v1/logs). A firewall rule change may have blocked the connection.
5
Restore the pipeline and confirm events are flowing
After fixing the root cause, monitor Splunk for 15 minutes to confirm events are being received. Check for any event backfill from the gap period.
HR / Manager Answer
How to explain it
A SIEM blind spot for identity events is a P1 security incident — not a P2 technical issue. Six hours of missing identity logs means six hours of undetected potential attacks. I restore the pipeline urgently and add monitoring so blind spots are detected in under 5 minutes going forward.
🏢 Real-World Example
🏢 SOC Blind for 8 Hours — Splunk Token Had Expired at Midnight
BankingGroup SOC noticed at 8 AM: no Okta events in Splunk since midnight. API token for the Splunk-Okta integration had expired silently. 8 hours of missing identity logs. New token generated, Splunk updated, events restored. Added: Splunk alert if no Okta events received in 30 minutes = P1 alert. API token rotation on 90-day schedule now managed via the IAM token registry.
QP1M
How do you communicate a P1 identity incident to business leadership who are not technical?
P1L3 SeniorP1 Communication
▼
TICKETCOMM-P1-9308
PRIORITYP1
APPAll Systems
CLIENTCISO / Leadership
Troubleshooting Steps
1
Lead with business impact, not technical detail
The first sentence should say what is broken in business terms: 'Users cannot log into [app] — [X] employees are affected and cannot work.' Technical details come later.
2
Provide a clear status structure: Impact / Cause / Actions / ETA
Every update follows the same format: What is impacted, Why it happened (briefly), What we are doing right now, When we expect resolution. Leadership should never have to ask 'but what are you actually doing?'
3
Update every 15 minutes until resolved
Even if there is no progress: '15:30 update: Still investigating root cause. No change in status. Next update: 15:45.' Silence during a P1 makes leadership anxious and generates escalation calls.
4
After resolution: send a Root Cause Summary within 24 hours
Once resolved, send a clear one-page summary: what happened, how long it lasted, how many users were affected, what caused it, and what was done to prevent recurrence.
5
Never speculate on cause until confirmed
Do not say 'it might be X' in a leadership communication. Only state confirmed facts. Speculation that turns out to be wrong destroys credibility.
HR / Manager Answer
How to explain it
Leadership communications during a P1 should be calm, factual, impact-focused, and frequent. Technical details go to the engineering war room. Executives need business impact and ETA — not stack traces.
🏢 Real-World Example
🏢 P1 Communication Framework Praised by CISO During a 90-Minute Outage
FinancialServices IAM team built a P1 communication template after an earlier incident where the CISO had to call the IAM lead for updates every 10 minutes. New framework: impact-focused opener, 15-minute Slack updates, Root Cause Summary within 24 hours. During the next P1, the CISO commented: 'Best communications I have seen during an incident. I always knew what was happening and what you were doing.' Template adopted company-wide for all P1 incidents.
QP1N
A user reports suspicious emails being sent from their work account without their knowledge. How do you investigate from an IAM perspective?
P1 CompromiseL3 SeniorEmail Compromise
▼
TICKETSEC-EMAIL-9309
PRIORITYP1
APPEntra ID + Exchange
CLIENTSOC / IAM
Troubleshooting Steps
1
Check Entra ID Sign-in logs for the account for recent logins
Look for: logins from unfamiliar IPs or countries, logins at unusual hours, MFA downgrade or bypass events, or impossible travel.
2
Check if any mail forwarding rules were added
An attacker's first action after compromising an email account is often to add a forwarding rule to exfiltrate all incoming emails. Check Exchange/M365 for inbox rules created recently.
3
Revoke all sessions and force password reset
If a compromise is suspected: immediately revoke all active sessions and require a password reset. Do not wait for the investigation to complete.
4
Check for OAuth app consent granted by the user
Phishing attacks often trick users into consenting to a malicious OAuth app that has read access to email. Check Entra ID > Enterprise Apps > User consent for any suspicious app.
5
Remove unauthorized forwarding rules and revoke rogue app consent
Delete any forwarding rules the attacker created. Revoke any suspicious OAuth app consents. Check for other mailbox settings changes (OOO messages, delegate access).
6
Engage the SOC for a full email header analysis
Forward the suspicious sent emails to the SOC. Email header analysis will confirm whether the emails were sent from the user's compromised account or spoofed from outside.
HR / Manager Answer
How to explain it
Suspicious email activity often indicates account compromise or OAuth app abuse. I revoke sessions and reset the password immediately while the investigation runs — I don't wait to confirm compromise before acting.
🏢 Real-World Example
🏢 Executive Email Compromise: Forwarding Rule Sending All Emails to attacker@gmail.com
LawFirm executive reported clients receiving strange emails from her account. IAM checked: sign-ins from Romania (exec was in London), an inbox forwarding rule sending all mail to an external Gmail address, and an OAuth app 'MailHelper Pro' with Mail.ReadWrite consent granted 3 days ago. Actions: sessions revoked, password reset, forwarding rule deleted, OAuth consent revoked. Clients notified of potential email compromise. Phishing attack traced to a fake client portal link.
QP1O
You discover a shared admin account being used by multiple people — how do you remediate this?
P1 ComplianceL3Shared Account
▼
TICKETSEC-SHARED-9310
PRIORITYP1
APPOkta / AD Admin
CLIENTSecurity Governance
Troubleshooting Steps
1
Immediately identify all users who have access to the shared credentials
Check: who has the password (ask, check password vaults, check IT documentation). How many people have used this account? Pull all login history for the account.
2
Terminate the use of the shared account immediately — no grace period
Shared admin accounts cannot be audited — every action appears to come from the same identity. This is a compliance violation and security risk. No continued use while you fix it.
3
Create individual named admin accounts for each person
Each person who needs admin access gets their own named account. Admin accounts must be individual, non-shared, and tied to a real human identity.
4
Enable MFA on every new individual admin account before activation
Each new admin account must have MFA enrolled before being used. FIDO2 hardware key preferred for admin-level accounts.
5
Lock or disable the shared account and retain it for audit history
Do not delete the shared account immediately — its login history is part of the audit trail. Disable and retain for 90 days, then delete.
HR / Manager Answer
How to explain it
Shared admin accounts are a fundamental security and compliance violation — they make accountability impossible. I identify every person using the account, create individual named accounts for each, and disable the shared account immediately.
🏢 Real-World Example
🏢 Shared 'AdminAdmin' Account Found With 12 Users — SOX Audit Deficiency
ManufacturingPLC SOX audit found a shared 'sap-admin' account with 12 different people knowing the password. No audit trail — all SAP admin changes appeared to come from the same identity. 12 individual named SAP admin accounts created, MFA enforced on each. Shared account disabled. SOX finding noted as remediated. Policy added: any shared admin account = immediate P1 remediation. Shared accounts now prohibited in the IAM policy.
← Back to Okta Guide
💀
Interview Prep › P0 Critical Threats
P0 Critical Threats
P0 క్రిటికల్ బెదిరింపులు
Senior L3 / Incident Command
QP0A
Security detects valid OAuth tokens being reused from different geolocations without MFA prompts — Token Replay Attack.
Valid OAuth tokens different geolocations నుండి MFA prompts లేకుండా reuse అవుతున్నాయని Security detect చేసింది — Token Replay Attack.
P0 BreachL3 SeniorToken ReplayCAE
▼
TICKETSEC-P0-ID-90871
PRIORITYP0
APPOkta, Entra ID, M365
CLIENTSOC / CISO Office
Troubleshooting Steps
ట్రబుల్‌షూటింగ్ దశలు
1
Revoke all refresh tokens tenant-wide
Tenant-wide అన్ని refresh tokens revoke చేయండి
This is the fastest way to force re-authentication. In Entra ID: Revoke Sign In Sessions. In Okta: Clear User Sessions for all affected users.
This fastest way to force re-authentication. Entra ID: Revoke Sign In Sessions. Okta: affected users కోసం Clear User Sessions.
2
Force sign-out across all apps
అన్ని apps లో force sign-out చేయండి
Ensure the token revocation propagates to all connected applications. Microsoft CAE helps here.
Token revocation అన్ని connected applications కు propagate అవుతుందని ensure చేయండి. Microsoft CAE ఇక్కడ helps.
3
Enable Continuous Access Evaluation (CAE)
Continuous Access Evaluation (CAE) enable చేయండి
CAE ensures that token revocation is enforced in real time across supporting apps — tokens are no longer valid the moment they're revoked.
CAE token revocation supporting apps లో real time లో enforce అవుతుందని ensures — revoke అయిన moment లోనే tokens invalid.
4
Investigate source and scope
Source మరియు scope investigate చేయండి
Analyze token issuance timestamps and geolocation logs. Identify which users are affected and which apps issued long-lived tokens.
Token issuance timestamps మరియు geolocation logs analyze చేయండి. Affected users identify చేసి long-lived tokens issue చేసిన apps identify చేయండి.
5
Reduce token lifetime and enforce device binding
Token lifetime తగ్గించి device binding enforce చేయండి
Reduce access token lifetime to 1 hour and refresh token lifetime. Enforce device-bound tokens — tied to a specific enrolled device.
Access token lifetime 1 గంటకు తగ్గించండి. Device-bound tokens enforce చేయండి — specific enrolled device కు tied.
HR / Manager Answer
HR / Manager సమాధానం
How to explain it
ఎలా వివరించాలి
A token replay attack is a P0 — an active breach. I revoke all tokens immediately, which forces everyone to re-authenticate. Then I enable CAE for real-time enforcement. My long-term fix reduces token lifetimes and enforces device binding so stolen tokens are useless even if extracted.
Token replay attack P0 — active breach. అన్ని tokens వెంటనే revoke చేసి everyone ని re-authenticate force చేస్తాను. CAE real-time enforcement కోసం enable చేస్తాను. Long-term fix token lifetimes తగ్గించి device binding enforce చేస్తుంది — stolen tokens extract చేసినా useless.
Real-World Organizational Example
నిజ జీవిత సంస్థ ఉదాహరణ
🏢 Org Example 🏢 సంస్థ ఉదాహరణ  SaaS Company P0: CFO Session Token Stolen via Browser Extension  Browser Extension ద్వారా CFO Session Token Stolen
FinTechPlatform SOC detected at 3 AM: the CFO's valid Microsoft 365 token was being used from two locations simultaneously — their home in Austin and an IP in Eastern Europe. The CFO was asleep. P0 declared. Actions: (1) Revoked all refresh tokens for the CFO and top 20 executives in 4 minutes. (2) Forced sign-out from all M365 apps. (3) Enabled CAE for M365 apps. Investigation found a malicious browser extension had silently exfiltrated the session token from the CFO's work laptop. Long-term fix: (a) Reduced token lifetime to 1 hour. (b) Blocked browser extension installation via Intune policy. (c) Deployed device-bound token requirement for executive accounts. No data exfiltration confirmed beyond the initial token theft.
FinTechPlatform SOC రాత్రి 3 AM కు detect చేసింది: CFO's valid M365 token రెండు locations నుండి simultaneously use అవుతోంది — Austin home మరియు Eastern Europe IP. CFO నిద్రపోతున్నారు. P0 declared. Actions: (1) 4 నిమిషాల్లో CFO మరియు top 20 executives refresh tokens revoke. (2) M365 apps నుండి force sign-out. (3) M365 apps కు CAE enable. Investigation: malicious browser extension CFO work laptop నుండి silently session token exfiltrate చేసింది. Long-term: (a) Token lifetime 1 గంటకు తగ్గింపు. (b) Intune policy ద్వారా browser extension installation block. (c) Executive accounts కు device-bound token requirement.
QP0B
A corrupted HR data feed updates thousands of users with an incorrect department, triggering excessive access across all apps.
Corrupted HR data feed thousands of users department incorrectly update చేసింది, అన్ని apps లో excessive access trigger అయింది.
P1 HIPAAL3GovernanceHR Feed
▼
TICKETSEC-P1-HR-54002
PRIORITYP1
APPWorkday + Okta + Entra ID
CLIENTHR & Compliance
Troubleshooting Steps
ట్రబుల్‌షూటింగ్ దశలు
1
Pause HR imports and lifecycle workflows
HR imports మరియు lifecycle workflows pause చేయండి
Immediately disable the Workday → Okta import schedule. Stop all lifecycle automation triggered by the corrupted data.
Workday → Okta import schedule వెంటనే disable చేయండి. Corrupted data trigger చేసిన lifecycle automation అన్నీ stop చేయండి.
2
Roll back recent access changes
Recent access changes roll back చేయండి
If Okta has captured the group/app assignment changes from the bad import, revert them. Export current state first as a backup.
Bad import నుండి Okta group/app assignment changes capture చేసి ఉంటే revert చేయండి. ముందు current state backup గా export చేయండి.
3
Revoke access tied to incorrect attributes
Incorrect attributes తో tied access revoke చేయండి
Identify which apps received access grants based on the wrong department attribute. Revoke those grants immediately.
Wrong department attribute ఆధారంగా access grants receive చేసిన apps identify చేసి వెంటనే revoke చేయండి.
4
Identify and correct HR source data
HR source data identify చేసి correct చేయండి
Work with HR to identify the corrupted data, correct it in Workday, and validate before re-enabling imports.
HR తో corrupted data identify చేసి Workday లో correct చేయండి, re-enabling imports కు ముందు validate చేయండి.
5
Add validation controls
Validation controls add చేయండి
Implement change threshold alerts: if > 5% of users change a critical attribute in a single import, pause and require human approval.
Change threshold alerts implement చేయండి: single import లో > 5% users critical attribute change అయితే pause చేసి human approval require చేయండి.
HR / Manager Answer
HR / Manager సమాధానం
How to explain it
ఎలా వివరించాలి
A corrupted HR feed causing mass access changes is a governance emergency. I stop the data pipeline immediately, roll back the bad changes, and work with HR to fix the source data before resuming. Long-term, I add threshold validation so bulk changes can never be applied without human review.
Corrupted HR feed mass access changes cause చేయడం governance emergency. Data pipeline వెంటనే stop చేసి, bad changes roll back చేసి, HR తో source data fix చేసిన తర్వాత resume చేస్తాను. Long-term, bulk changes human review లేకుండా apply కాకుండా threshold validation add చేస్తాను.
Real-World Organizational Example
నిజ జీవిత సంస్థ ఉదాహరణ
🏢 Org Example 🏢 సంస్థ ఉదాహరణ  Healthcare HR Feed Bug Grants Billing Access to Clinical Staff  HR Feed Bug వల్ల Clinical Staff కు Billing Access
RegionalHealthSystem had a Workday integration that updated department codes overnight. A data migration error changed all nurses' department code from 'CLINICAL' to 'BILLING'. Okta group rules then automatically added 800 nurses to the Billing Applications group, granting access to billing software and financial dashboards — a HIPAA violation. Discovery: A nurse noticed a Billing App appeared on her Okta dashboard. IAM woken at 5 AM. Response: Paused all HR imports in 3 minutes. Revoked Billing group access for 800 users in 7 minutes. Workday data corrected in 2 hours. Imports resumed with correct data. Added: if > 50 users change department in one import, pause for manual review. Incident reported to compliance as potential HIPAA exposure.
RegionalHealthSystem Workday integration overnight department codes update చేస్తోంది. Data migration error అన్ని nurses' department code 'CLINICAL' నుండి 'BILLING' గా changed. Okta group rules automatically 800 nurses ని Billing Applications group కు add చేసి billing software మరియు financial dashboards access — HIPAA violation. Discovery: Nurse తన Okta dashboard లో Billing App కనుగొంది. IAM 5 AM కు wake. Response: 3 నిమిషాల్లో HR imports pause. 7 నిమిషాల్లో 800 users Billing group access revoke. 2 గంటల్లో Workday data correct. Added: single import లో > 50 users department change అయితే manual review కోసం pause.
QP0C
B2B guest user gains internal app access via group nesting — privilege escalation. How do you respond?
B2B guest group nesting ద్వారా internal access పొందారు. ఎలా respond?
P0L3 SeniorB2B Guest
▼
TICKETSEC-P0-B2B-11904
PRIORITYP0
APPEntra ID B2B
CLIENTSecurity / Legal
Troubleshooting Steps
1
Disable guest account immediately
Guest account disable
Suspend/disable external guest account NOW. Don't investigate first — remove access first.
External guest account NOW suspend/disable. Investigate తర్వాత.
2
Remove from all groups
All groups remove
Remove guest from every group — dynamic and static. Check nested group inheritance.
Every group — dynamic + static. Nested inheritance check.
3
Audit what was accessed
Accessed content audit
Review Entra ID + app audit logs for the access duration. What data was viewable?
Entra ID + app audit logs. What data viewable?
4
Identify root cause: group nesting
Root cause: nesting
Which dynamic rule allowed the guest? Was it an OR condition or missing userType filter?
Which dynamic rule? OR condition or missing userType filter?
5
Fix: add userType eq Member to all internal groups
Fix: userType filter
Add condition: userType eq 'Member' to all internal dynamic groups. Blocks guests by design.
All internal dynamic groups: userType eq 'Member' condition. Blocks guests.
6
Quarterly B2B access reviews
Quarterly reviews
Review all guest access quarterly. Auto-disable guests inactive for 90 days.
Quarterly review. 90-day inactive auto-disable.
HR / Manager Answer
How to explain it
External privilege escalation requires immediate containment — disable first, investigate second. Then fix the group architecture.
External privilege escalation: disable first, investigate second, group architecture fix.
Real-World Organizational Example
🏢 Law Firm Guest Views Confidential Client Files via Group OR Condition
LegalPartners LLP external auditor B2B guest added to 'LLP-Finance-Staff' dynamic group — rule had OR condition that matched 'Auditor' job title. Guest had access to confidential client SharePoint files for 11 days. Audit showed: viewed 3 files, no download. All internal groups now have userType eq 'Member' condition. Quarterly B2B reviews implemented.
LegalPartners auditor guest — OR condition dynamic rule. 11 days finance files access. Viewed 3 files. userType eq 'Member' fix all groups. Quarterly B2B reviews.
QP0D
SCIM provisioning loop — users added and removed from apps every few minutes. How do you fix?
SCIM provisioning loop — users few minutes add/remove. ఎలా fix?
P1SCIM Loop
▼
TICKETINC-P1-SCIM-55640
PRIORITYP1
APPOkta + SCIM apps
CLIENTIT Ops
Troubleshooting Steps
1
Pause SCIM immediately
SCIM pause
Disable SCIM provisioning integration for affected apps. Stops the loop.
Affected apps SCIM disable. Loop stop.
2
Freeze lifecycle workflows
Lifecycle freeze
Disable Okta Workflows triggering SCIM. Isolate the source.
Okta Workflows disable. Source isolate.
3
Identify conflicting attribute ownership
Ownership conflict identify
Are two systems both writing the same attribute? Write-write conflict = loop.
Two systems same attribute writing? Write-write conflict = loop.
4
Find circular group dependencies
Circular dependencies
Does group change trigger attribute update → group re-eval → more group changes? Feedback loop.
Group change → attr update → group re-eval → more changes? Feedback loop.
5
Fix and re-enable one app at a time
One app at a time
Define authoritative source. Break dependencies. Re-enable one app, monitor 30 min, then next.
Authoritative source define. Dependencies break. One app, 30 min monitor, next.
HR / Manager Answer
How to explain it
A SCIM loop destabilizes access for hundreds of users. I stop it immediately, find the write-write conflict or circular dependency, and restart carefully.
SCIM loop destabilizes access. Stop immediately, conflict find, restart carefully.
Real-World Organizational Example
🏢 400 Users Flickering: Two Systems Fighting Over Department Code
GlobalManufacturer 400 users' ServiceNow access flickering every 8 minutes. Two systems both writing 'department': Workday format 'Engineering', IT legacy format 'ENG001'. Each system overwrote the other — classic write-write conflict. Paused legacy SCIM writes. Declared Workday authoritative. Loop stopped instantly. Attribute ownership matrix added to IAM runbook.
GlobalManufacturer 400 users 8-min flicker. Workday 'Engineering' vs IT 'ENG001'. Write-write conflict. Legacy SCIM pause. Workday authoritative. Instant stop.
QP0F
A zero-day vulnerability is announced in Okta's platform. How do you assess and respond?
P0 AssessmentL3 SeniorZero-Day Response
▼
TICKETSEC-ZERODAY-9401
PRIORITYP0 Assessment
APPOkta
CLIENTCISO / IAM
Troubleshooting Steps
1
Immediately read Okta's security advisory in full
Access the Okta Security Advisories page. Read the full advisory: what is the vulnerability, what is the attack vector, which product versions are affected, and what is Okta's recommended mitigation.
2
Determine if your org is in the affected scope
Not every Okta vulnerability affects every customer. Determine: does the vulnerability affect your Okta org type (Classic vs Identity Engine), your configuration, or specific features you use?
3
Check for indicators of compromise in your tenant
Even before patching: search your Okta System Log for any signs of exploitation. Look for anomalous admin activity, new API tokens created, policy changes, or authentication from unknown IPs.
4
Apply Okta's recommended mitigations immediately
Follow Okta's advisory instructions exactly. Mitigations may include: disabling specific features, revoking API tokens, enabling additional monitoring, or applying configuration changes.
5
Brief the CISO and maintain hourly status updates
Zero-day vulnerabilities in identity infrastructure require CISO-level visibility. Provide hourly updates: current exposure, mitigation steps taken, and any findings from the compromise check.
6
Monitor Okta's communications for patch deployment
Okta deploys patches to their cloud infrastructure. Monitor their status page for patch deployment confirmation. After patch: re-verify your configuration and run another compromise check.
HR / Manager Answer
How to explain it
A zero-day in Okta is an organization-wide risk because every application is connected through it. I read the advisory in full, assess our specific exposure, check for signs of exploitation, apply mitigations, and maintain active communication with the CISO.
🏢 Real-World Example
🏢 Okta Support Case Zero-Day (2023): 134-Customer Impact — Rapid IAM Response
During a real Okta zero-day involving their support case system, IAMTeam immediately reviewed the advisory, checked their tenant for any admin actions taken via the support channel in the prior 2 weeks, and confirmed no unauthorized changes. Rotated all API tokens as a precautionary measure. Briefed CISO within 30 minutes of the advisory going public. Full tenant audit completed within 4 hours. No compromise found. Post-incident: Okta admin activity monitoring alert added to SOC.
QP0G
A ransomware attack has encrypted file servers and the attacker is attempting to pivot to Active Directory. How does the IAM team respond?
P0 RansomwareL3 SeniorAD Hardening
▼
TICKETSEC-RANSOMWARE-9402
PRIORITYP0
APPActive Directory + Entra ID
CLIENTCISO / SOC
Troubleshooting Steps
1
Immediately isolate Active Directory domain controllers from the network if possible
If the attack is still in progress and DCs are not yet encrypted, isolate them from the network to prevent the attacker from reaching them. This is a drastic step that requires CISO approval.
2
Reset all privileged account passwords immediately — starting with Domain Admins
The attacker's goal is to compromise a Domain Admin account. Reset every Domain Admin, Enterprise Admin, and Schema Admin password immediately, even before the attack vector is fully identified.
3
Revoke all Kerberos tickets by resetting the KRBTGT account password — twice
If the attacker has Domain Admin, they may have created Golden Tickets. Reset the KRBTGT account password twice (24 hours apart) to invalidate all existing Kerberos tickets in the domain.
4
Check for new admin accounts or group membership changes in the last 24 hours
Run: Get-ADUser -Filter {WhenCreated -gt (Get-Date).AddHours(-24)} and check Domain Admin group membership changes. Attackers create backdoor admin accounts before deploying ransomware.
5
Review Entra ID for any cloud-only accounts created or permissions elevated
If the attacker reached Entra ID Connect, they may have created cloud-only Global Admin accounts. Review all admin account creation in the last 48 hours in Entra ID.
6
Coordinate with SOC on the incident timeline — do not act unilaterally
Every IAM action during an active attack must be coordinated with the SOC Incident Commander. Uncoordinated actions can destroy forensic evidence or tip off the attacker.
HR / Manager Answer
How to explain it
During a ransomware attack, the IAM team's role is to sever the attacker's access paths to privileged accounts and Active Directory. Speed is critical but coordination with the SOC is essential — uncoordinated actions can destroy forensic evidence.
🏢 Real-World Example
🏢 Ransomware Attack: IAM Resets Saved Domain Controllers From Encryption
ManufacturingGroup ransomware attack on a Friday night. SOC detected lateral movement toward domain controllers. IAM team (on-call): reset all Domain Admin passwords in 12 minutes, reset KRBTGT twice, revoked all admin sessions. Attackers' path to the DCs was cut. 4 file servers encrypted, but all domain controllers survived intact. Active Directory recovered without restoration from backup. KRBTGT double-reset was the key action that prevented a full domain compromise.
QP0H
An attacker has created a persistent backdoor admin account in Okta. How do you find and remove it?
P0 BackdoorL3 SeniorOkta Tenant
▼
TICKETSEC-BACKDOOR-9403
PRIORITYP0
APPOkta
CLIENTSOC / CISO
Troubleshooting Steps
1
Pull a full list of all admin accounts and compare against your known baseline
Export all Okta admin accounts (Super Admins, Org Admins, Read-Only Admins). Compare against the approved admin list. Any account not on the approved list is a suspect.
2
Check the creation date and creator of every admin account
In Okta System Log, search for admin role assignment events. Backdoor accounts are typically created at odd hours (2-4 AM) by the compromised admin account.
3
Deactivate the backdoor account immediately — do not delete yet
